newsSince: Silergy posted strong March 2026 results (revenue NT$1.78B, +12% YoY; net profit attributable +97% YoY) and analysts model ~41% EPS / ~22% revenue annual growth, reflecting a power-IC cyclical recovery. The stock is up ~22% since June 9 and ~248% YTD. Recovery is real but in general power-management/consumer-industrial ICs, not in CPO/HBM/advanced-packaging bottlenecks.

sources.label: Silergy Gen4 ramps early as AI/auto demand drive 2026 growth (Digitimes)
Silergy Gen4 ramps early as AI/auto demand drives 2026 growth >20% (DigiTimes)

verdicts.rankRationale: On the CPO lens Silergy is a power-IC analog name with weak direct co-packaged-optics/HBM/advanced-packaging relevance; prior case flagged it for removal. No CPO-relevant news since June 9, while +248% YTD worsens the asymmetry. Down on this page; thesis fit remains poor.
verdicts.bull: High-growth analog/power-IC franchise; Q1 net income more than doubled, ~40% EPS CAGR modeled.
verdicts.neutral: Strong business, wrong page — minimal CPO/optical-engine exposure.
verdicts.bear: +248% YTD with no CPO linkage; consumer/industrial cycle risk and steep valuation.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Valuation; consumer/industrial cycle; no CPO bottleneck role
